subtitle: Connect any LLM to manage your project — create tables, endpoints, indexes, and more.
fullDesc: Connect any LLM to your DataForge project. Create, modify, and optimize your database schema and API endpoints through natural language.

rest.label: REST Gateway
rest.fullDesc: Compatible with GPT, Claude, Codex, Gemini, and any LLM. Send HTTP requests to manage your project.

mcp.label: MCP Server
mcp.fullDesc: Model Context Protocol for Claude Code, Cursor, Claude Desktop. AI connects directly and manages your project.

studio.label: AI Studio
studio.fullDesc: Create AI-powered endpoints with custom prompts, multiple providers, response validation, and conversation context.

get_project_info.name: get_project_info
get_project_info.desc: Learn about DataForge platform, its capabilities, and how to work with this project

get_schema_context.name: get_schema_context
get_schema_context.desc: Get the full project schema: all tables, columns, types, indexes, foreign keys, and API endpoints

create_table.name: create_table
create_table.desc: Create a new database table with columns, UUID primary key, and timestamps

alter_columns.name: alter_columns
alter_columns.desc: Modify table structure: add new columns, change types, rename, or drop existing columns

drop_table.name: drop_table
drop_table.desc: Delete a table with all its data, indexes, foreign keys, and associated endpoints

add_index.name: add_index
add_index.desc: Create an index on columns to speed up queries. Supports btree, hash, gin, gist types

drop_index.name: drop_index
drop_index.desc: Remove an index by name when it's no longer needed or hurts write performance

add_foreign_key.name: add_foreign_key
add_foreign_key.desc: Add a foreign key constraint to enforce referential integrity between tables

drop_foreign_key.name: drop_foreign_key
drop_foreign_key.desc: Remove a foreign key constraint by its name

create_endpoint.name: create_endpoint
create_endpoint.desc: Create an API endpoint — table-based CRUD or custom SQL with parameters, auth, caching

update_endpoint.name: update_endpoint
update_endpoint.desc: Update an existing endpoint's query, auth type, cache settings, rate limits

execute_sql.name: execute_sql
execute_sql.desc: Run read-only SQL (SELECT/EXPLAIN/WITH) for data analysis, debugging, and optimization

list_cron_jobs.name: list_cron_jobs
list_cron_jobs.desc: List all scheduled cron jobs with their status, schedule, and last run info

get_cron_job.name: get_cron_job
get_cron_job.desc: Get cron job details including configuration and recent execution history

create_cron_job.name: create_cron_job
create_cron_job.desc: Create a scheduled cron job to run SQL queries on a recurring schedule

update_cron_job.name: update_cron_job
update_cron_job.desc: Update a cron job's name, schedule, query, or active status

delete_cron_job.name: delete_cron_job
delete_cron_job.desc: Delete a cron job permanently with all its run history

toggle_cron_job.name: toggle_cron_job
toggle_cron_job.desc: Pause or resume a cron job without deleting it

run_cron_job.name: run_cron_job
run_cron_job.desc: Execute a cron job immediately to test it, returns the result

setup.title: Setup Guide
setup.step1desc: Go to Settings → API Tokens → Create a token with admin permissions.
setup.step2restDesc: Copy the text below and paste it to your AI (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.):
setup.step2restPrompt:
I want you to manage my DataForge database project via REST API.

Base URL: {{url}}
Auth header: x-api-key: YOUR_TOKEN_HERE

Available endpoints:
- GET /info — learn about DataForge platform, capabilities, data types, best practices
- GET /context — get full project schema (tables, columns, indexes, foreign keys, endpoints)
- POST /tables — create table (body: {name, columns[], add_uuid_pk, add_timestamps})
- PUT /tables/:name/columns — alter columns (body: {changes: [{action: add|alter|drop|rename, name, ...}]})
- DELETE /tables/:name — drop table with all data and endpoints
- POST /tables/:name/indexes — add index (body: {columns[], type: btree|hash|gin|gist, is_unique})
- DELETE /tables/:name/indexes/:indexName — drop index
- POST /tables/:name/foreign-keys — add FK (body: {source_column, target_table, target_column, on_delete, on_update})
- DELETE /tables/:name/foreign-keys/:constraintName — drop FK
- POST /endpoints — create API endpoint (body: {method, path, source_type, source_config, auth_type, cache_enabled, cache_ttl, rate_limit})
- PUT /endpoints/:id — update endpoint
- DELETE /endpoints/:id — delete endpoint
- POST /execute-sql — run read-only SQL (body: {query})

Start by calling GET /info to learn about the platform, then GET /context to see the current schema.
setup.step2mcpTitle: Add MCP config to your AI client
setup.step2mcpDesc: Copy this JSON and add it to your AI client's MCP settings:
setup.step2mcpClients: Supported clients: Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code
setup.step3mcpDesc: Ask your AI: "Get the schema of my DataForge project"
setup.waitingDesc: Activity will appear here once your AI makes the first request

plugins.restDesc: Universal REST API for any LLM — GPT, Claude, Codex, Gemini, local models.
plugins.mcpDesc: Direct integration via MCP protocol — Claude Code, Cursor, Claude Desktop.
plugins.studioDesc: Create AI-powered endpoints with custom prompts, response validation, and conversation context.

studio.selectEndpoint: Select an endpoint or create a new one
studio.deleteDesc: This will permanently delete this AI endpoint, all its logs and context sessions.

form.name: Name
form.systemPromptPlaceholder:
Example prompt structure:

ROLE:
You are [role]. Your task is [task].

INPUT FORMAT:
You will receive [what kind of data].

RESPONSE FORMAT (JSON only, nothing else):
{
  "result": true/false,
  "category": "category name" or null,
  "reason": "short explanation"
}

RULES:
1. [Rule one]
2. [Rule two]
3. ...

IMPORTANT:
- Respond ONLY with valid JSON
- If unclear — return negative result
- [Other edge cases]
form.retryDesc: Re-ask AI if response fails validation
